automotive-ev-battery.title: ICCT analysis maps global distribution of EV sales
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A new International Council on Clean Transportation study reveals that over 20 million battery electric vehicles and plug-in hybrids were deployed globally by 2025, with emerging economies adding approximately one million new electric vehicles—double the 2024 figure. The analysis demonstrates accelerating EV adoption beyond traditional markets in China, Europe, and North America.

automotive-ev-battery.title: California launches $1 billion rebate programme for electric trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: California has opened applications for a new rebate programme providing more than $1 billion in incentives through 2030 for electric medium- and heavy-duty trucks. The initiative aims to accelerate commercial fleet electrification and enhance California's competitive position in the zero-emission vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Xiaomi sets up subsidiary for battery and electric motor production
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Xiaomi has established Beijing Xiaomi Jingxu Technology to produce electric motors and batteries, including plans for a joint-venture battery factory with 15 GWh annual capacity expected to commence production later this year. The expansion marks Xiaomi's vertical integration into battery and powertrain manufacturing for its EV operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ford plans new compact EV and additional models for Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ford announced plans to introduce five entirely new passenger car models by end of 2029, all manufactured and marketed in Europe, including a fully electric compact car, a small electric SUV, and three models with multiple powertrain options. The strategy represents a significant commitment to expanding Ford's European EV portfolio.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Gotion receives €92 million for two battery projects in Spain
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery manufacturer Gotion High-Tech has been provisionally awarded €92 million under Spain's Perte VEC programme to develop battery production and recycling facilities in Valladolid. The projects include a cathode production facility and a battery recycling plant, strengthening European battery supply chain infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Green SM to deploy more than 18,000 VinFast EVs across the Philippines
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Vietnamese taxi operator and leasing provider Green SM has announced plans to deploy up to 18,497 VinFast electric vehicles across the Philippines in what could represent the largest bulk deal for VinFast EVs in the country. The deployment reflects growing momentum for EV adoption in Southeast Asian emerging markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Jaguar's electric four-door GT to be named Type 01
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Jaguar has announced that its new all-electric four-door grand tourer will be called the Type 01, with the '0' symbolizing electric propulsion and zero emissions, and '1' representing the first vehicle in Jaguar's new era. The naming follows the recent release of camouflaged prototype photos as part of the brand's EV transition strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: USA: BMW introduces discounted charging rates at Ionna network
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW has launched a preferred pricing programme for BMW and Mini electric vehicle drivers at Ionna charging network locations across the United States, offering a 20 percent discount on charging sessions accessible through Plug & Charge and the My BMW App. The initiative enhances charging affordability and integration for BMW's EV customer base.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Go-Ahead London deploys 13 Enviro100EV
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Go-Ahead London has introduced 13 battery-electric Alexander Dennis Enviro100EV buses on Transport for London route 322 operating between Crystal Palace and Clapham Common. The deployment contributes to London's ongoing transition toward zero-emission public transportation infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Nidec to withdraw from e-axle business
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Japanese industrial company Nidec is seeking to exit its e-axle business, planning to withdraw from a 2019 joint venture with GAC in China and from a European joint venture with Stellantis. The withdrawal reflects competitive pressures and potential consolidation in the electric drivetrain component supply sector.

world-news.title: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
world-news.summary: The US president announced he is postponing a planned attack on Iran scheduled for Tuesday, citing that serious negotiations are now underway. The decision reflects diplomatic pressure from Gulf state allies and marks a potential de-escalation in US-Iran tensions.

world-news.title: US suspends joint defence effort with Canada dating back to World War II
world-news.summary: The Trump administration has suspended a longstanding bilateral defense partnership with Canada that has existed since World War II. The suspension reflects the administration's broader criticism of allied nations for allegedly failing to meet mutual defense obligations.

world-news.title: Death toll from Israeli strikes on Lebanon passes 3,000, officials say
world-news.summary: The death toll from Israeli military strikes on Lebanon has exceeded 3,000 since March, marking a significant escalation despite a nominal ceasefire agreement. The conflict with the Lebanese armed group Hezbollah continues to cause substantial civilian casualties.

world-news.title: At least 100 dead in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, official says
world-news.summary: The World Health Organization has declared an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo an international emergency, with confirmed deaths exceeding 100. The declaration underscores the global health threat posed by the viral outbreak.

world-news.title: Clashes as Morales-allied protesters march on Bolivian capital
world-news.summary: Bolivia is experiencing significant civil unrest as protesters aligned with former president Evo Morales march on the capital amid an ongoing economic crisis. The clashes between demonstrators and security forces reflect growing political instability under President Paz's government.

world-news.title: Is the US trying to force regime change in Cuba?
world-news.summary: As the US blockade on Cuba intensifies an ongoing fuel crisis, the United States is offering humanitarian aid while simultaneously attempting to dictate conditions for Cuba's political future. The policy raises questions about US intervention in Cuban internal affairs.

world-news.title: Venezuelan protesters clash with police at rally for political prisoners
world-news.summary: Venezuelan demonstrators clashed with police during a rally advocating for the release of political prisoners, underscoring ongoing political tensions in the country. The incident reflects continued civil unrest regarding human rights concerns in Venezuela.

world-news.title: Selling children to survive: Afghan fathers forced to make impossible choices
world-news.summary: Afghanistan faces a severe humanitarian crisis as poverty rates have reached critical levels, with approximately three in four people unable to meet basic needs. The desperate economic situation has driven some families to consider child labor and exploitation as survival measures.

world-news.title: Four killed in protests during Kenyan strikes over high fuel prices
world-news.summary: Four people were killed during protests in Kenya as thousands of commuters were stranded due to strikes triggered by escalating fuel costs. The unrest highlighted growing economic grievances across the country, with major disruptions to transportation in the capital Nairobi.

world-news.title: Trump administration unveils $1.7bn fund to compensate his allies as he drops tax lawsuit
world-news.summary: The Trump administration has established a $1.7 billion compensation fund for individuals alleging they were subjected to unfair investigations, while simultaneously withdrawing a tax-related lawsuit. The settlement represents a significant policy reversal and use of federal resources.

science-space.title: NASA's MAVEN Makes 1st Discovery of Atmospheric Effect at Mars
science-space.summary: Scientists using NASA's MAVEN mission have identified an unprecedented atmospheric phenomenon in Mars' upper atmosphere previously only observed in Earth's magnetosphere, where charged particles are compressed in a toothpaste-like mechanism. This discovery, made in December 2023, represents the first confirmed observation of this type of atmospheric effect on the Red Planet and advances understanding of Mars' atmospheric dynamics.

science-space.title: Scientists found stardust trapped in Antarctic ice. What could it tell us about our solar system?
science-space.summary: Researchers have discovered interstellar dust particles preserved in Antarctic ice that penetrated the solar system's heliosphere and reached Earth. This finding provides a rare opportunity to analyze pristine extraterrestrial material and gain insights into the composition and dynamics of material entering our solar system.

science-space.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
science-space.summary: SpaceX's 34th commercial resupply mission launched on a Falcon 9 rocket carrying nearly 6,500 pounds of scientific experiments and supplies to the International Space Station. The mission represents continued collaboration between NASA and SpaceX in supporting ongoing research and operations aboard the orbiting laboratory.

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4893-4899: Drilling at Campo Marte and a Visit From the Psyche Spacecraft
science-space.summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover has resumed drilling operations after freeing its arm from a trapped rock, targeting a new site that represents the same geological stratum as the previous Atacama block within a layered sulfate unit. The drilling campaign continues to advance scientific understanding of Mars' subsurface composition and geology.

science-space.title: Watch Vega C rocket launch European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit tonight
science-space.summary: A Vega C rocket successfully launched the SMILE space weather mission, a joint European-Chinese satellite designed to observe solar activity and its effects on Earth's magnetosphere. The mission represents significant international cooperation in advancing space weather monitoring capabilities.

science-space.title: Beacon of Light
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured a striking image of galaxy M77's active core, where intense gravitational forces from the central black hole heat infalling gas to extreme temperatures, causing it to emit brilliant radiation. This observation provides detailed insight into the physics of supermassive black hole accretion and active galactic nuclei.

science-space.title: NASA Selects Next Class of Space Health Postdoctoral Fellows
science-space.summary: The Translational Research Institute for Space Health has selected two early-career scientists to conduct postdoctoral research focusing on space food systems and astronaut eye health. These fellows will develop biomedical, behavioral, and technological approaches relevant to human spaceflight and long-duration missions.

science-space.title: This amazing NASA video shows the exact moment the Artemis 2 Orion capsule broke free of its service module, and we can't stop watching it
science-space.summary: NASA released dramatic video footage documenting the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft's separation from its service module just before atmospheric entry on April 10, 2026. The visualization provides unprecedented detail of this critical spacecraft separation event during the lunar mission.

science-space.title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
science-space.summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has imaged an unusual galaxy displaying characteristics of both elliptical and spiral morphologies, partially obscured by dust filaments and clumps. This observation captures a galaxy undergoing structural transition, offering clues to the mechanisms driving galactic evolution.

science-space.title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
science-space.summary: A comprehensive analysis has mapped changes in artificial night-time light levels across Earth, identifying regions where light pollution has intensified and where it has decreased. The analysis provides crucial data for understanding environmental changes and the impact of human activity on nocturnal ecosystems.

finance-markets.title: Mortgage costs rise sharply on Middle East conflict
finance-markets.summary: Mortgage rates have increased significantly across North America and Europe amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, despite central banks maintaining current interest rate levels. The rise in home loan costs reflects broader market concerns about inflation and regional instability affecting financial markets.

finance-markets.title: Bond Yield Surge Poses Threat to Asia's AI-Fueled Stock Rally
finance-markets.summary: Rising US bond yields are threatening Asia's equity market gains as inflation concerns offset enthusiasm for artificial intelligence-driven growth. The yield surge highlights tension between technology sector optimism and macroeconomic headwinds facing regional markets.

finance-markets.title: US eases Russian oil sanctions in bid to contain Iran price surge
finance-markets.summary: The United States has renewed a 30-day licence easing sanctions on Russian oil in an effort to control surging fuel prices that are pressuring American consumers. The move reflects Washington's attempt to balance geopolitical concerns with domestic economic priorities.

finance-markets.title: The US megadeal set to spark a fight over the cost of the AI boom
finance-markets.summary: The proposed merger between NextEra and Dominion would consolidate control of US 'data centre alley,' intensifying debate over the infrastructure costs and utility pricing implications of artificial intelligence expansion. The deal is expected to trigger regulatory scrutiny and industry concerns about AI-driven energy demands.

finance-markets.title: Oil Slips on Iran Optimism, Asian Stocks Waver: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices declined after President Trump signalled a pause in military action against Iran, supporting optimism for diplomatic resolution and restored energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz. Asian equity markets showed mixed performance amid broader economic uncertainty.

finance-markets.title: Korea's World-Beating Market Rally Hinges on a Handful of Stocks
finance-markets.summary: South Korea's outperforming stock market is becoming concentrated among a small group of companies, raising concerns about market overheating and concentration risk. Weak market breadth suggests that gains are not broadly distributed across the index.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX IPO set to hand $20bn stake to one hedge fund
finance-markets.summary: D1 Capital Partners and other major Wall Street firms stand to receive substantial stakes in SpaceX if the rocket maker successfully completes its initial public offering next month. The anticipated listing could value SpaceX's investor positions at approximately $20 billion.

finance-markets.title: Standard Chartered to cut 15% of back-office jobs as AI use escalates
finance-markets.summary: Standard Chartered plans to eliminate 15% of its back-office positions as the Asia-focused lender accelerates artificial intelligence implementation under chief executive Bill Winters' growth strategy. The restructuring reflects broader banking industry trends toward automation and workforce optimization.

finance-markets.title: Analog Devices Is Said to Near $1.5 Billion Deal for Empower
finance-markets.summary: Analog Devices is in advanced negotiations to acquire privately-held Empower Semiconductor for approximately $1.5 billion in cash. The acquisition would expand Analog Devices' semiconductor portfolio and technological capabilities.

finance-markets.title: Musk loses case against OpenAI after two hours of jury deliberations
finance-markets.summary: Elon Musk's legal challenge against OpenAI was rejected by a jury in a swift two-hour deliberation, providing a significant victory for CEO Sam Altman. The ruling removes a notable obstacle to OpenAI's planned public offering.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic has acquired the dev tools startup used by OpenAI, Google, and Cloudflare
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has acquired Stainless, a New York-based startup founded in 2022 that specializes in automating the creation and maintenance of software development kits (SDKs). The acquisition strengthens Anthropic's position in the AI infrastructure space by integrating tools used by major technology companies including OpenAI, Google, and Cloudflare.

tech-ai.title: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI
tech-ai.summary: A California jury unanimously ruled against Elon Musk in his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI, determining that his claims were barred by the statute of limitations. The verdict concludes one of the tech industry's most high-profile legal battles regarding OpenAI's shift from nonprofit to for-profit operations.

tech-ai.title: NYC Health + Hospitals says hackers stole medical data and fingerprints during breach affecting at least 1.8 million people
tech-ai.summary: New York's public healthcare system disclosed a major cybersecurity breach affecting at least 1.8 million people, with hackers stealing personal data, medical records, and biometric information including fingerprints. The breach ranks among the largest recorded in 2026 and raises significant privacy and security concerns for a major healthcare provider.

tech-ai.title: SandboxAQ brings its drug discovery models to Claude — no PhD in computing required
tech-ai.summary: SandboxAQ is integrating its AI-powered drug discovery models with Anthropic's Claude to democratize access to advanced computational biology tools. The move positions accessibility as a key differentiator as venture-backed competitors like Chai Discovery and Isomorphic Labs race to build superior drug discovery AI models.

tech-ai.title: Open source tool maker Grafana Labs says hackers stole its code, refuses to pay ransom
tech-ai.summary: Grafana Labs disclosed a security breach in which hackers stole its codebase and threatened to publish the source code unless the company paid a ransom. The incident highlights ongoing cybersecurity risks to prominent open-source infrastructure providers.

tech-ai.title: South Korea's LetinAR is building optics behind AI glasses
tech-ai.summary: South Korean startup LetinAR is developing compact optical components that could serve as the foundational lens technology for the emerging AI glasses market. The company's thumbnail-sized lens represents a significant advancement in making practical augmented reality and AI-enabled eyewear feasible.

tech-ai.title: Kin Health raises $9M to build an AI notetaker for patients
tech-ai.summary: Kin Health has secured $9 million in funding to develop an AI-powered application that records doctor visits and generates summaries with recommended next steps, which patients can share with family and friends. The platform extends AI notetaking technology from business meetings into the healthcare sector to improve patient engagement and information retention.

tech-ai.title: Amazon's new Alexa+ powered feature can generate podcast episodes
tech-ai.summary: Amazon has expanded its Alexa+ subscription service to include AI-powered podcast generation, enabling users to create custom audio content on demand. The feature represents Amazon's broader pivot toward positioning Alexa as a personalized AI content platform rather than a traditional voice assistant.

tech-ai.title: OSHA probing worker death at SpaceX's Starbase site
tech-ai.summary: The Occupational Safety and Health Administration is investigating a worker death at SpaceX's Starbase facility in Texas, adding to a pattern of safety concerns at the site. Starbase has recorded a higher injury rate compared to other SpaceX facilities, raising questions about workplace safety practices.

tech-ai.title: LG will release the first 1000Hz, 1080p gaming monitor this year
tech-ai.summary: LG has announced the UltraGear 25G590B, the first gaming monitor capable of achieving a native 1000Hz refresh rate at 1080p resolution, addressing the traditional trade-off between refresh rate and display resolution. The 24.5-inch IPS monitor is designed for competitive esports players who demand both high framerates and adequate pixel density.

health-biotech.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Medicare drug price negotiation
health-biotech.summary: The Supreme Court has declined to review multiple cases brought by pharmaceutical companies challenging Medicare drug price negotiation provisions under the Inflation Reduction Act. This decision strengthens the regulatory framework for federal drug price negotiations and may weaken remaining pharma industry lawsuits on the matter.

health-biotech.title: Maryland state affordability board places a price cap on Ozempic
health-biotech.summary: Maryland's state prescription drug affordability board has set price limits on Ozempic, marking the first such action by one of nine states pursuing drug affordability initiatives. This development signals growing state-level efforts to control costs for high-demand medications.

health-biotech.title: FDA approves AstraZeneca's baxdrostat for certain patients with hypertension
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has approved AstraZeneca's baxdrostat, a novel hypertension treatment representing a key product in the company's strategy to reach $80 billion in revenue by 2030. The drug works by inhibiting a blood-pressure-raising enzyme and addresses an important unmet medical need in hypertension management.

health-biotech.title: Merck's ADC sac-TMT gets its first global Phase 3 win ahead of schedule
health-biotech.summary: Merck announced an early positive Phase 3 trial for sac-TMT, an antibody-drug conjugate developed with China-based Kelun-Biotech, in endometrial cancer treatment. This success represents a critical pipeline victory for Merck as it pursues its goal of $70 billion in annual sales by the next decade.

health-biotech.title: With no approved vaccine for Ebola outbreak, experts weigh testing a long shot
health-biotech.summary: As an Ebola outbreak accelerates in the Democratic Republic of Congo, health experts are considering emergency testing of an experimental vaccine with unknown efficacy levels. This public health response reflects the urgent nature of the outbreak and limited preventive options available.

health-biotech.title: White House taps Amazon, GoodRx, and Mark Cuban to bolster TrumpRx
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration's TrumpRx drug discount platform has expanded to include 600 generic medications through partnerships with major online pharmacies including Amazon and GoodRx. The initiative aims to improve access and affordability of generic prescription medications for American consumers.

health-biotech.title: Regeneron's Phase 3 skin cancer miss adds to mounting failures in LAG-3
health-biotech.summary: Regeneron's LAG-3 inhibitor fianlimab has failed a late-stage skin cancer trial, continuing a troubling trend of clinical setbacks for the LAG-3 drug class. This disappointing result compounds challenges for Regeneron's pipeline and the broader immunotherapy sector.

health-biotech.title: Amgen stands by rare disease drug Tavneos amid Japan liver toxicity report
health-biotech.summary: Amgen is defending its rare disease drug Tavneos despite mounting regulatory challenges, including an FDA recommendation for market withdrawal and European investigations into data integrity concerns. A Japanese company distributing the drug has warned physicians against new prescriptions due to liver toxicity reports.

health-biotech.title: Pharmalittle: U.S. biotech's China problem creates industry divisions
health-biotech.summary: The biotech industry faces internal tensions over engagement with Chinese pharmaceutical firms, with competing views on whether to partner with or treat them as strategic rivals. This strategic divide reflects broader geopolitical concerns affecting industry partnerships and innovation strategies.

health-biotech.title: Regeneron reaches $125M Parabilis deal for hard-to-catch drug targets
health-biotech.summary: Regeneron has partnered with Parabilis Medicines in a deal valued at up to $2.2 billion to develop therapies targeting difficult-to-address molecular pathways. The strategic partnership represents Regeneron's efforts to diversify its pipeline amid recent setbacks in its cancer drug portfolio.

crypto-web3.title: SEC to propose tokenized stock framework as Wall Street efforts deepen
crypto-web3.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is reportedly poised to release a major proposal to institute a digital assets framework for tokenized stocks. This regulatory development signals growing institutional acceptance and legitimacy of blockchain-based securities.

crypto-web3.title: SEC's innovation exemption for tokenized stocks to come as early as this week
crypto-web3.summary: The SEC has approved several entities including the NYSE and Nasdaq to move forward with tokenized stock initiatives under an innovation exemption. This development represents a significant regulatory milestone enabling major financial institutions to launch blockchain-based trading products.

crypto-web3.title: Solana is shedding its memecoin reputation as big banks move billions into its ecosystem
crypto-web3.summary: Wall Street and major payment providers are quietly deploying billions of dollars onto the Solana network for tokenized funds and global payments infrastructure, according to Messari research. This institutional capital influx signals a fundamental shift in how traditional finance views the blockchain platform.

crypto-web3.title: Zerohash secures first EMI license under MiCA for stablecoin and brokerage services in Europe
crypto-web3.summary: Zerohash Europe became the first firm licensed under Europe's MiCA regulation to hold both full Electronic Money Institution (EMI) status and crypto compliance simultaneously. This regulatory achievement demonstrates the integration of traditional and digital finance oversight under Europe's flagship crypto framework.

crypto-web3.title: Mike Novogratz's Galaxy receives New York BitLicense for institutional crypto push
crypto-web3.summary: Galaxy Digital secured approval for a New York BitLicense, becoming the second company this year to obtain the coveted credential for offering digital asset services in the state. The license expands Galaxy's regulatory footprint to over 50 jurisdictions worldwide.

crypto-web3.title: Hyperliquid's USDC deal could supercharge HYPE, pressure Circle, Coinbase margins
crypto-web3.summary: Hyperliquid's revenue-sharing arrangement with USDC could redirect approximately $160 million in annual revenue from Coinbase and Circle to its ecosystem, according to Compass Point analysts. The deal positions Hyperliquid as a formidable competitor in decentralized finance revenue generation.

crypto-web3.title: Minnesotan banks and credit unions set to provide crypto custody August 1
crypto-web3.summary: Minnesota established the Midwest's first unified digital asset safety framework enabling banks and credit unions to provide cryptocurrency custody services beginning August 1. The regulatory development broadens institutional access to crypto asset management infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Stripe-backed Tempo taps $7.5 billion DeFi lender Morpho to expand beyond payments
crypto-web3.summary: Tempo partnered with Morpho, a major decentralized lending protocol, to integrate onchain yield and lending capabilities into its payments-focused blockchain. The collaboration aims to create a comprehensive onchain finance platform for companies building decentralized applications.

crypto-web3.title: Revolut wants to take Dogecoin mainstream with its new physical payment card
crypto-web3.summary: European fintech Revolut launched a physical debit card enabling users to spend Dogecoin anywhere Visa and Mastercard are accepted without exchange fees. The initiative reflects growing mainstream adoption of cryptocurrency payment infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Bitwise to add HYPE to balance sheet using fees from Hyperliquid ETF
crypto-web3.summary: Asset manager Bitwise announced plans to acquire Hyperliquid's native token using fee revenue from its Hyperliquid ETF product. Hyperliquid has surpassed both Ethereum and Solana in weekly blockchain fee generation, driven primarily by its perpetual futures trading activity.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anduril Leads Varied Lineup Of Large Deals
startups-vc.summary: Defense tech unicorn Anduril Industries secured $5 billion in funding, marking the largest financing round of the week amid a diverse portfolio of deals across robotics, space tech, biotech, and physical infrastructure applications. The funding activity reflects continued investor appetite for companies addressing real-world problems beyond software.

startups-vc.title: Meridian Ventures launched a $35M fund with a focus on MBA-deferred founders
startups-vc.summary: Meridian Ventures closed a $35 million fund targeting early-stage founders building enterprise technology, with sector-agnostic investment spanning fintech, logistics, healthcare, and AI applications. The fund aims to back non-traditional founders bypassing traditional MBA pathways to launch ventures.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator W15 graduate, completed its initial public offering after building a digital operating system for the construction industry across 373 locations nationwide. The milestone represents a significant exit for the YC network and validates the company's mission-driven approach to modernizing construction operations.

startups-vc.title: South Korea's LetinAR is building optics behind AI glasses
startups-vc.summary: South Korean startup LetinAR has developed thumbnail-sized optical lenses that could become foundational technology for the emerging AI glasses industry. The breakthrough in miniaturized optics addresses a key technical challenge in wearable AI device development.

startups-vc.title: Figma Beats, Raises and Accelerates! Boom! Stock Rockets 12%! But … It Still Trades for Less than 10X ARR
startups-vc.summary: Figma delivered strong Q1 results with accelerating revenue growth, improved net dollar retention, and successful AI monetization that exceeded market expectations, driving a 12 percent stock price increase. The company's financial performance demonstrates that AI features can effectively drive incremental revenue in mature SaaS platforms.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator announced Harshita Arora as a new General Partner, bringing deep expertise in fintech and infrastructure from her background as a serial founder. Her appointment strengthens YC's investment capabilities in critical infrastructure and financial technology sectors.

startups-vc.title: Tired vs. Wired: $4 Trillion in IPOs Coming, $100B in M&A, Vibing Isn't Killing Salesforce After All, and Why the B2B Doomers Were Dead Wrong (Mostly)
startups-vc.summary: Analysis of 2025-2026 B2B SaaS trends reveals contrary-to-prediction outcomes including sustained CRM value, robust M&A activity exceeding $100 billion, and an anticipated $4 trillion in upcoming IPOs despite earlier pessimistic forecasts. The data suggests structural bullishness in enterprise software markets despite AI disruption concerns.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Law Firm Operating System, Building Defense Tech Near The Battlefield, And Cell-Based Milk
startups-vc.summary: A curated selection of notable recent startup investments highlights emerging opportunities in physical-world applications including legal tech infrastructure, defense manufacturing platforms, and cellular agriculture. These deals reflect investor focus on solving tangible industry problems rather than digital-only solutions.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Quantum Computing Startup Investment Slows In 2026 While Public Markets Hold Strong
startups-vc.summary: Quantum computing startup funding is tracking toward a decline from 2025 peaks despite continued deal activity and large individual rounds, while public quantum companies maintain investor confidence. The pattern suggests market consolidation and selectivity among quantum technology ventures.

startups-vc.title: Schmoozing Is Dead, Agents Are Hitting 120% of Humans, and Growth Is the Only Thing That Matters: 10 Things From the Closing Q&A at SaaStr AI Annual 2026
startups-vc.summary: SaaStr AI Annual 2026 closing session highlighted key industry themes including AI agents outperforming human benchmarks, the diminishing role of relationship selling, and growth as the primary success metric in modern B2B software. Discussions reflected the current inflection point in enterprise AI adoption and sales methodology transformation.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until recently. The leaked archive included sensitive information about CISA's software development, testing, and deployment processes, representing one of the most significant government data leaks in recent history.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
cybersecurity.summary: A cross-site scripting vulnerability (CVE-2026-42897) in Microsoft Exchange is actively being exploited to compromise Outlook Web Access mailboxes, with no patch currently available. The zero-day presents an immediate threat to organizations using the affected Exchange systems.

cybersecurity.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems
cybersecurity.summary: A new Windows privilege escalation zero-day vulnerability designated MiniPlasma has been disclosed that allows attackers to achieve SYSTEM-level privileges even on fully patched Windows systems through the Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver. The proof-of-concept release by security researcher Chaotic Eclipse follows previous disclosures of YellowKey and GreenPlasma Windows flaws.

cybersecurity.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
cybersecurity.summary: Supply chain attackers have launched coordinated campaigns against npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub within a 48-hour window, targeting credentials and secrets from developer environments including API keys, cloud credentials, and SSH tokens. The attacks demonstrate how compromised developer access can undermine the integrity of trusted software packages and infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
cybersecurity.summary: A confluence of critical security incidents occurred this week involving an active Exchange mail server flaw, poisoned trusted packages, fake AI model pages distributing stealers, and exploitation of network control systems. The incidents illustrate a pattern where compromised dependencies and leaked credentials can cascade into broader cloud infrastructure compromise.

cybersecurity.title: 'CanisterWorm' Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
cybersecurity.summary: A financially motivated threat group has deployed a self-propagating worm that exploits poorly secured cloud services and wipes data from systems configured with Iran's time zone or Farsi language settings. The attack represents an attempt to inject geopolitical objectives into cybercriminal operations.

cybersecurity.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
cybersecurity.summary: Multiple enterprise software vendors including Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, and n8n have released patches for critical vulnerabilities enabling remote code execution, SQL injection, and privilege escalation attacks. The critical Ivanti Xtraction flaw (CVE-2026-8043, CVSS 9.6) poses particular risk for information disclosure and client-side attacks.

cybersecurity.title: Four Malicious npm Packages Deliver Infostealers and Phantom Bot DDoS Malware
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers identified four malicious npm packages totaling nearly 3,000 downloads that distribute information-stealing malware and DDoS botnet code, including a clone of the Shai-Hulud worm. The discovery underscores ongoing supply chain risks in the popular JavaScript package repository.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian technology firm specializing in DDoS protection was found to be enabling botnet attacks against competing network operators, with the firm's leadership attributing the malicious activity to a security breach perpetrated by rivals. The incident raises questions about the integrity of security service providers.

cybersecurity.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
cybersecurity.summary: INTERPOL coordinated a regional cybercrime operation involving 13 Middle East and North Africa countries that resulted in 201 arrests and identified 382 additional suspects between October 2025 and February 2026. The initiative disrupted malicious infrastructure and represented a significant enforcement effort against organized cybercrime in the region.

energy-climate.title: Factcheck: US and Iran are world's only major emitters without net-zero targets
energy-climate.summary: A Carbon Brief analysis reveals that the United States and Iran are the only major global emitters without official net-zero emission targets, a factual correction to claims that the UK is an outlier on climate commitments. The finding underscores significant gaps in global climate policy among major economies.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: UK no longer top UN Green Climate Fund donor after latest aid cut
energy-climate.summary: The United Kingdom has relinquished its position as the leading contributor to the UN's Green Climate Fund following recent reductions in climate finance allocations. The shift reflects changing priorities in global climate financing and impacts the Fund's capacity to support developing nations' climate adaptation efforts.

energy-climate.title: UK grants development consent for offshore wind farms
energy-climate.summary: The UK government has approved development consent for the Dogger Bank South and North Falls offshore wind farm projects, representing significant expansion of the nation's offshore wind capacity. These approvals advance the UK's renewable energy infrastructure and support its decarbonization objectives.

energy-climate.title: Spain, France set benchmark in renewables expansion without biodiversity loss
energy-climate.summary: Research comparing France, Italy, and Spain demonstrates that France and Spain have developed more advanced frameworks for integrating biodiversity protection into renewable energy planning under the European Green Deal. The study identifies significant gaps in cumulative environmental impact assessment across all three nations, highlighting the need for stronger ecological integration in renewable expansion.

energy-climate.title: DOE exceeded its authority with coal retirement delay, states tell appeals court
energy-climate.summary: Multiple U.S. states are challenging a Department of Energy decision that delayed coal power plant retirements, arguing the agency overstepped its legal authority in the Consumers Energy case. The appellate court decision could establish precedent for future legal challenges to federal interventions in fossil fuel plant operations.

energy-climate.title: JinkoSolar, Masdar sign 2GW PV module supply agreement for RTC project
energy-climate.summary: JinkoSolar and Abu Dhabi's Masdar have entered into a supply agreement for 2 gigawatts of Tiger Neo photovoltaic modules to support the RTC renewable energy project. The agreement reflects continued expansion of utility-scale solar capacity in the Middle East region.

energy-climate.title: Japanese scientists achieve world record 25.14% efficiency for perovskite-CIGS tandem solar cell
energy-climate.summary: Researchers in Japan have achieved a certified world record efficiency rate of 25.14% for perovskite-CIGS tandem solar cells, as validated by Japan's National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology. The breakthrough demonstrates significant progress in next-generation solar cell technology with potential implications for commercial solar deployment.

energy-climate.title: ASL selects AGL, Enel X for 532MW of new NSW firming capacity
energy-climate.summary: AusEnergy Services Limited has contracted two firming projects in New South Wales with combined capacity of 532 megawatts through partnerships with AGL and Enel X. The selection addresses Australia's need for reliable grid firming capacity to support renewable energy integration.

energy-climate.title: GCL Technology expands beyond PV with new energy storage business
energy-climate.summary: GCL Technology is diversifying its operations by entering the lithium-ion battery storage sector based on LFP materials as part of a broader pivot toward a multi-product renewable energy platform. The expansion addresses growing global demand for energy storage solutions to complement photovoltaic capacity.

energy-climate.title: Toyo denies Ethiopia duty evasion, confirms 4 GW ramp and U.S. cell plant
energy-climate.summary: Japanese solar manufacturer Toyo has dismissed allegations of duty evasion related to its Ethiopian solar cell facility while confirming the site reached 4 gigawatts of capacity in the past year. The company also announced plans to establish a new onshore solar cell manufacturing plant in the United States.

asia-pacific.title: The Trump-Xi Summit's Nuclear Shadow Over Asia
asia-pacific.summary: China's accelerating nuclear weapons development is creating strategic strain on the United States' extended deterrence commitments across Asia, particularly concerning to American allies in the region. The Trump-Xi summit discussions are occurring against this backdrop of fundamental shifts in the nuclear balance that threaten to reshape security architectures throughout the Asia-Pacific.

asia-pacific.title: Trump's China Opening and Japan's Taiwan Anxiety
asia-pacific.summary: Japan expresses concern about potential diplomatic realignment as the United States recalibrates its relationship with China, fearing Tokyo may be marginalized in crucial Asia-Pacific negotiations. The shift in Washington-Beijing dynamics raises questions about security commitments and strategic priorities in the region.

asia-pacific.title: Duterte vs Marcos: The Political Feud Tearing Apart the Philippines
asia-pacific.summary: An escalating political conflict between former and current Philippine leadership extends beyond domestic politics to potentially influence the nation's stance on South China Sea territorial disputes. The internal division could significantly impact regional stability and maritime governance in contested waters.

asia-pacific.title: Kim Jong Un Convenes a Military-Wide Commander Meeting
asia-pacific.summary: North Korea's leadership has ordered significant restructuring of the military's training systems and reinforcement of border forces along the Korean Peninsula, signaling intensified security posturing. The moves reflect Pyongyang's broader strategy to reshape the regional security landscape amid elevated tensions.

asia-pacific.title: Taiwan's Special Defense Budget Cut Will Cost Its Drone Capabilities
asia-pacific.summary: Taiwan's legislative approval of a reduced defense budget will halt new domestic drone procurement at a critical juncture for the island's defense modernization efforts. The budgetary constraints come amid heightened security concerns in the Taiwan Strait and across the region.

asia-pacific.title: What's on the Agenda for the 3rd Lee-Takaichi Summit?
asia-pacific.summary: Japan and South Korea are moving to deepen institutionalization of their security, energy, and strategic cooperation frameworks, particularly in response to shifting geopolitical dynamics following recent US-China diplomatic engagement. The bilateral initiative seeks to strengthen regional stability amid broader Asia-Pacific realignment.

asia-pacific.title: China's firms have a glaring weakness as they face a barrage of EU probes
asia-pacific.summary: Chinese companies expanding overseas to compensate for domestic market pressures are encountering intensifying regulatory scrutiny from European authorities conducting multiple investigations. The compliance challenges represent a significant vulnerability as Beijing's firms pursue their international expansion strategy.

asia-pacific.title: Can PLA training catch up with China's rapid weapon development?
asia-pacific.summary: China's military leadership has issued a rare public warning that personnel training lags behind the pace of advanced weapons development, with some units showing inadequate integration between operators and sophisticated systems. The assessment indicates potential gaps in China's military readiness despite rapid hardware advancement.

asia-pacific.title: At SCO Security Heads Meeting, Russia Stakes Out 'Full-fledged Partnership' With Taliban-ruled Afghanistan
asia-pacific.summary: Russia is formalizing its security partnership with Taliban-controlled Afghanistan while explicitly opposing foreign military infrastructure in the country or neighboring states. The development signals Moscow's strategic interest in Central Asian stability and regional influence amid broader geopolitical competition.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong finance chief woos French wealth managers as European tour kicks off
asia-pacific.summary: Hong Kong's Financial Secretary is conducting a targeted European tour to attract global capital and reinforce the city's positioning as an international financial hub, meeting with major French wealth management institutions. The diplomatic initiative reflects Hong Kong's broader strategy to maintain financial competitiveness amid evolving geopolitical circumstances.
